top_signals.title: US Stocks Erase Drop as Traders Evaluate Middle East, Jobs Data
top_signals.summary: US stocks were muted on Tuesday as traders parsed the latest developments in the Middle East and fresh employment data.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Commodities regulator alleges Winklevosses' Gemini crypto exchange was politically targeted
top_signals.summary: CFTC chair Michael Selig said the agency's enforcement action against Gemini was part of a broader campaign against the crypto industry under President Biden.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: World Cup won’t boost US or European economies, experts warn
top_signals.summary: The upcoming Fifa World Cup is unlikely to deliver significant or lasting economic growth for its three hosts and the European market, experts have warned. The quadrennial tournament – being held across the United States, Canada and Mexico – is set to “generate excitement far more reliably than sustained economic transformation”. top_signals.title: Rare Yamazaki whisky sells for record $1 million
top_signals.summary: Bottled exclusively for a club in Nagoya and autographed by Suntory's chief blender, the 50-year-old Japanese whisky sold for almost twice its estimate.
